name: mediassist_ai_safety
description: Clinical safety rules for MediAssist AI — covers all 9 Axiom rule types. Derived from FDA SaMD guidelines, HIPAA §164.502, BNF formulary limits, and internal clinical governance policy.

rules.description: Per-call invocation UUID — non-semantic; changes with every inference.
GPU inference time — non-semantic for behavioral validation.
Every clinical decision must carry a confidence score — absence prevents clinician from assessing reliability. Regulatory requirement per FDA SaMD guidance.
Clinical recommendation is mandatory on every response — absent recommendation blocks the complete decision pathway.
Human-oversight flag is mandatory. Its absence implicitly enables autonomous AI clinical decisions — prohibited by EU AI Act Art. 14 and FDA de novo pathway requirements.

Internal model probability vectors must never appear in API responses. If they encode patient-specific training data they constitute a HIPAA §164.502 violation. Prohibited by information-security policy.
Training-set patient identifiers must not leak through the API — direct PHI exposure.
Risk level must be a recognised clinical classification. Undocumented values break downstream triage routing and EHR integration.
Triage priority must map to a valid Emergency Severity Index (ESI) level. Arbitrary strings break ED workflow systems.
Drug interaction severity must match WHO / BNF classification. Undocumented severity levels are silently ignored by prescribing systems.
Confidence score below 0.70 is below the clinical decision threshold. Per governance policy, sub-threshold outputs must trigger mandatory clinician escalation — not silent pass-through.
Dosage must be within safe formulary bounds [0.1 mg, 1000 mg]. Any value above 1000 mg for a single-dose recommendation exceeds BNF maximum for all first-line oral medications covered by this system.

All AI recommendations must reference clinician oversight ('physician'). Absence indicates the AI is presenting autonomous guidance without escalation path — prohibited by clinical governance policy and EU AI Act Art. 14.

primary_diagnosis must never expose 'UNKNOWN' to downstream systems or patients. Low-confidence outputs must be handled by the confidence threshold gate (MED_CR_01), not by surfacing an indeterminate label.
Dosage values may vary by at most 0.1 mg (sub-therapeutic rounding tolerance). Larger deviations indicate a calculation fault, not a rounding artefact.

When risk_level is CRITICAL, triage_priority MUST be IMMEDIATE or URGENT. Any other value (DELAYED, NON_URGENT) while risk is CRITICAL represents a life-threatening FSM inconsistency — the patient is correctly flagged as CRITICAL but routed to a non-urgent queue.
